rationale: Both correctly diagnose the config/tokenizer mismatch, cite the same crash site, and reproduce the confirmed workaround and closure disposition — closely matching the thread. X is more careful: it sticks to what's verifiable, ties its concrete tolerant-guard code fix directly to the crash site, and doesn't introduce unverified claims. Y adds a useful config table but asserts PR #2713 'introduced' the ratio-slice logic (the thread only credits #2713 with forcing the two-stage topology) and claims the fix was confirmed 'on NPU hardware,' neither of which is supported by the given evidence.
